content.text:
Here are some of the **top AI headlines as of Thursday, April 9, 2026** that stood out across major company and industry sources:

1. **OpenAI says it raised $122 billion in new funding**  
   OpenAI announced on **March 31, 2026** that it closed a funding round with **$122 billion in committed capital** at an **$852 billion post-money valuation**, framing it as fuel for the “next phase of AI.” ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/accelerating-the-next-phase-ai//?utm_source=openai))

2. **OpenAI acquires TBPN, an AI-focused media/community platform**  
   On **April 2, 2026**, OpenAI said it had acquired **TBPN**, saying the move would help “accelerate the global conversation around AI” and expand how it communicates with builders and the broader ecosystem. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/openai-acquires-tbpn?utm_source=openai))

3. **Google launches Veo 3.1 Lite for cheaper AI video generation**  
   Google announced **Veo 3.1 Lite** on **March 31, 2026**, describing it as its most cost-effective video generation model, available through the **Gemini API** and **Google AI Studio**. Google also said it would reduce pricing for **Veo 3.1 Fast** on **April 7**. ([blog.google](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/ai/veo-3-1-lite/?utm_source=openai))

4. **Microsoft pushes enterprise AI with a new “Frontier Suite”**  
   Microsoft said on **March 9, 2026** that it was launching a new enterprise AI bundle centered on **Microsoft 365 Copilot**, **Agent 365**, and expanded model access including **Claude** and “next-gen OpenAI models.” It also announced general availability timing and pricing for new enterprise AI offerings. ([blogs.microsoft.com](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/03/09/introducing-the-first-frontier-suite-built-on-intelligence-trust/?utm_source=openai))

5. **Google continues rolling out Gemini-based consumer AI features**  
   Google said in late March it was adding new **Gemini** features to **Google TV**, continuing the broader expansion of AI-generated news summaries and assistant features across consumer products. ([blog.google](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/platforms/google-tv/new-gemini-features-march-2026/?utm_source=openai))

If you want, I can also turn this into a **“top 10 AI news today” roundup with brief summaries and links from mainstream outlets like Reuters, The Verge, and TechCrunch**.
